zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Bourne於1627年9月23日在英國斯塔福爾拉普利受洗時，她的父親John Bourne26歲，母親Frances Hill17歲。她於1647年之前與John Welles在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1668年10月8日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州費艾費爾德史達科特享年41歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州費艾費爾德史達科特笛卡爾 從雷加納爾 波英 格洛德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Bourne于1627年9月23日在英国斯塔福爾拉普利受洗时，她的父亲John Bourne26岁，母亲Frances Hill17岁。她于1647年之前与John Welles在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1668年10月8日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州費艾費爾德史達科特享年41岁，埋葬在美国康涅狄格州費艾費爾德史達科特笛卡爾 從雷加納爾 波英 格洛德。

nameMeanings.name: Bourne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived beside a stream, from southern Middle English bourne, Old English burna, burne ‘spring, stream’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example Bourn in Cambridgeshire or Bourne in Lincolnshire. In surnames the reference is often to an old stream called burna, surviving as the name of a farm. This word was replaced as the general word for a stream in southern dialects by Old English brōc (see Brook ) and came to be restricted in meaning to a stream flowing only intermittently, especially in winter.

variants.name: Burn
meanings: English (Northumberland and Durham): variant of   Bourne  .

variants.name: Born
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Heinz, Helmut, Berthold, Frieda, Friedrich, Fritz, Gerhard, Horst, Jutta.
English (of Norman origin): nickname from Old French borgne ‘one-eyed, squint-eyed’.

variants.name: Cockburn
meanings: Scottish (Midlothian) and northern English: habitational name from a place in Berwickshire named Cockburn, from Old English cocc ‘cock, rooster’ (or the related byname Cocca) + burna ‘stream’ (see Bourne ). This surname is traditionally pronounced Coburn.

variants.name: Bournes
meanings: English: variant of Bourne with plural or post-medieval exrescent -s.

variants.name: Brook
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a brook, stream, water-meadows or low, marshy ground, from Middle English brook, Old English brōc ‘brook, stream’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, such as Brook (Kent, Rutland) or Brooke (Norfolk).
North German: topographic name for someone who lived by a water meadow or marsh, from Low German brook (cognate with German Bruch ‘swamp’ and Old English brōc; see 1 above).
Americanized form of Dutch Broek: topographic name from broek, a cognate of 2 above.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Bourne was christened on 23 September 1627, in Lapley, Staffordshire, England, her father, John Bourne, was 26 and her mother, Frances Hill, was 17. She married John Welles before 1647, in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 8 October 1668, in Stratford, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 41, and was buried in Old Congregational Burying Ground, Stratford, Fairfield, Connecticut, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Bourne a été baptisée le 23 septembre 1627, à Lapley, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, John Bourne, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Frances Hill, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé John Welles avant 1647, à Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 octobre 1668, à Stratford, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterrée à Old Congregational Burying Ground, Stratford, Fairfield, Connecticut, États-Unis.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Bourne è stato battezzato il 23 settembre 1627 nel Lapley, Staffordshire, England, sua padre, John Bourne, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Frances Hill, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato John Welles prima del 1647, nel Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 8 ottobre 1668, nel Stratford, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 41 anni, e fu sepolta a Old Congregational Burying Ground, Stratford, Fairfield, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bourneが1627年9月23日にイギリススタフォードシュアイアラプリーで洗礼を受けた時，父John Bourneは26歳，母Frances Hillは17歳であった。1647年以前にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドでJohn Wellesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1668年10月8日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ファイルフィールドストラトフォードで41歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ファイルフィールドストラトフォードオルド コングレガティオナル ブリイング グロウンドにて埋葬された。

ko.Darien, Stamford, Fairfield, Connecticut: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 파어필드 스타퍼드 더리
ko.Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colo: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드
ko.Old Congregational Burying Ground, Strat: 미국 코네티컷 주 파어필드 스트라트퍼드 드 콘그레가티오널 버이잉 그라운드
ko.Stratford, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 파어필드 스트라트퍼드
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bourne(이)가 1627년 9월 23일 영국 스태퍼드실르 라플리에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Bourne(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Frances Hill(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1647년 이전 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드에서 John Welles(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1668년 10월 8일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 파어필드 스트라트퍼드에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 파어필드 스트라트퍼드 드 콘그레가티오널 버이잉 그라운드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Bourne foi batizada em 23 de setembro de 1627, em Lapley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Bourne, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Frances Hill, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com John Welles antes 1647, em Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de outubro de 1668, em Stratford, Fairfield, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 41 anos, e foi sepultada em Old Congregational Burying Ground, Stratford, Fairfield, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Bourne была крещена 23 сентября 1627 г. в местности лапли, стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Bourne было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Frances Hill, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Welles перед 1647 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 8 октября 1668 г. в местности стратфорд, феирфиелд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 41 год и была похоронена в местности олд конгрегатиональ буринг гроунд, стратфорд, феирфиелд, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Bourne se bautizó el 23 de septiembre de 1627, en Lapley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Bourne, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Frances Hill, tenía 17 años. Se casó con John Welles antes de 1647, en Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 8 de octubre de 1668, en Stratford, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 41 años, y fue sepultada en Old Congregational Burying Ground, Stratford, Fairfield, Connecticut, Estados Unidos.
